plugins.description: [POC] PreToolUse(Task|Agent) observation hook + SessionStart observation-log snapshot, implemented in hexa-lang (`_subagent_route.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). v0.1.0 is observation-only — it does NOT deny, NOT rewrite. On every Task/Agent fire it dumps `tool_input` (key list + agent_type + prompt preview + isolation) to `~/.sidecar/subagent-route.log.jsonl` (capped 100 lines, jsonl format mirroring pool-route's route-log) and emits a non-blocking `additionalContext` advisory suggesting the equivalent `ssh <host> -- claude --print --no-tty -p '<prompt>'` rewrite. SessionStart event reads the last 5 observations back as additionalContext so the model sees recent subagent fires without recon. PoC purpose — probe the actual tool_input payload shape at runtime (Claude Code docs leave Agent/Task tool_input fields under-specified vs Bash), and probe the `additionalContext` UX before a real-rewrite v0.2.0. Always exits 0; advisory only.

plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and considers a worktree only when its branch is in that merged set OR its HEAD commit truly landed on origin/main. 0.2.0 adds FOUR unconditional live-work guards so an active worktree is NEVER wiped mid-task: (a) dirty-tree (uncommitted changes → SKIP), (b) recent-mtime (HEAD commit or any file modified < 1h → SKIP), (c) cwd-in-use (a live process' cwd is in the worktree, via lsof → SKIP), (d) HEAD-ancestor real-merged check (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <wt-HEAD> origin/main`) — a vanished `origin/<branch>` ref alone is IGNORED so a reused branch name can't condemn fresh work. (e) Atomic prune: `git worktree remove` is the sole removal path; on failure NOTHING is deleted (no partial / manual rm). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs → short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: sidecar-auto-sync
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that runs `sidecar sync` once per Claude Code session, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_auto_sync.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the gap between an upstream `git push` on origin/main and the local plugin cache — no more manual `sidecar sync` between sessions. Silent on success; brief stderr advisory on failure. Skipped silently only on a real precondition: the `sidecar` binary not on PATH (fresh machine without `hx install sidecar` yet). Adds ~1-2s to session startup (network pull + cache copy + installed_plugins.json patch). NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) — no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for load-assessment commands (project.tape @D s9). Fires when a Bash command looks like a Mac load check — `uptime` (word) · `loadavg` / `vm.loadavg` (substring) · `ps -A` adjacency or `ps -Ao pcpu` · `top -l ` (macOS) / `top -b ` (Linux) · `getloadavg`. Emits non-blocking additionalContext reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks — pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) — the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: step-by-step
plugins.description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook with TWO modes (auto · manual default · 0.5.0 2-mode redesign) + 0.6.0 자동 4축 QA after ship (functional·visible·conformance·regression · regression FAIL → auto-revert · 나머지 alert · SKIP=PASS-equivalent · 결과는 plan.md `## qa-results`). MANUAL (the DEFAULT) — chat-form disambiguation: ONE question per round in chat (NOT AskUserQuestion), each round framed with the easy-mode 7-element scaffold (icon · canonical name · friendly alias · plain-line · everyday analogy · ASCII sketch · option compare-table · one-line recommendation) and closed with `→ A · B · 또는 자유응답` (free-form answers explicitly OK). Repeat round-by-round until ambiguity = 0, THEN render a `🎯 합의된 결정셋 (N개)` ASCII tree of every accumulated decision and PAUSE — user types `go` to confirm or `Qn=<other>` to flip ONLY that one (re-render after each flip). On `go`, write `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` (gitignored; frontmatter slug/mode/auto-weights/created + task brief + locked decisions + next-action checklist ending with a ship line + completion criteria) and LAUNCH A BACKGROUND AGENT (general-purpose, run_in_background=true) with a self-contained prompt — the agent runs end-to-end including ship + auto-QA 4축, no further user input needed; user can leave. AUTO — same chat-form scaffold rendered, but each question is AUTO-PICKED by the 4-axis weighted average instead of waiting for the user: 완성도 (complete · robust + edge-case coverage) + 단순 (simple · Occam) + 안전 (safe · blast radius minimized) + 표준 (std · fits the sidecar pattern), default 1:1:1:1, inline override `/sbs auto:safety <task>` (single-axis force) or `/sbs auto:complete=2,simple=3 <task>` (explicit weights). AUTO still pauses at the agreement screen so auto-picks get a user pre-commit checkpoint. Mid-run new ambiguity also opens a chat round + re-renders the agreement screen. Mode = FIRST arg token `auto`[:`<axis-or-weights>`] | `manual` (default manual). `legacy-manual` = the old per-step pause behavior — 1-version deprecation banner, use plain `manual` going forward. `go` is NOT a mode (use `/go` to continue the paused flow). ALL modes halt on a step failure (failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). 0.6.0 auto-QA 4축 (handoff agent ship 직후 자동 실행): functional (새 endpoint/verb 응답) · visible (URL/path/surface 노출) · conformance (locked decision ↔ 코드 1:1) · regression (기존 surface 미손상). Hybrid fail 정책 — regression FAIL만 auto-revert (`git revert <SHA> && git push && sidecar sync`) + banner, 나머지 3축은 ship 유지 + plan.md `## qa-deferred` append + banner. ALL PASS/SKIP → banner 없이 plan.md `## qa-results`에 ✓ 라인만 append.

plugins.name: mining
plugins.description: /mining — lens-driven DIVERGENCE (leaves) + connect-driven CONVERGENCE (edges) + tidy-driven ORGANIZE (phase regroup) workflow. Sibling 3rd pillar to /domain: <NAME>.md (snapshot) + <NAME>.log.md (step log) + <NAME>.mining.md (## cycles · ## leaves · ## edges, append-only) + <NAME>.mining.tape (idea cart of @X candidates). THREE workflows: **lens cycles** (divergence — depletion = 0 new leaves) · **connect cycles** (convergence — meaningful direct edges, transitive/re-packaging/generic-ancestor excluded; depletion = 0 new edges in a full pass) · **tidy** (organize — reorganize cycles into PHASE groups divergence/analysis/convergence/external + chronological cycle-index table; LOSSLESS — index preserves order so nothing can be lost). Bundled lens catalogue (extensible `~/.sidecar/lens/<name>.md`): **same-formula** · **ouroboros** (goal closure) · **dimensional** · **tension** · **combinatorial** · **custom**. Verbs (12): bare (status with auto-suggest tidy at ≥10 cycles & ≥500 lines) · `<lens>` (round) · `append` · `cycle new` · `depletion` · `tree` · `connect`/`edges` · `connect <a> <b>` · `graph` · `saturate` · `tidy`/`consolidate` [`--depth=light|full`] · `squash` (cosmetic dup-header dedup, low-risk pre-step before tidy --depth=full). Active-domain only (commons @D g58). Derived from demiurge RTSC's 15-cycle organic emergence + Cycle 15 phase-grouping reorg. Triggers — '/mining', '/mining <lens>', 'mining tree', 'mining connect', 'mining graph', 'mining saturate', 'mining tidy', 'mining consolidate', 'mining squash', 'lens 발산', '점잇기', '정리', 'phase 그룹', '마이닝 정리'.

plugins.name: micro-exp
plugins.description: /micro-exp [scope] — context-driven micro-experiment sweep orchestrator (domain-agnostic). Self-enumerate sweep candidates from the conversation / active domain (no manifest file — like /cycle) → Stage 1.5 per-candidate infra existence check (HALT to /cycle-bg when missing) → rent pods (≤budget) → copy inputs → run → Monitor (kind-appropriate alive-check) → on JOB DONE harvest+down + dispatch parse Agent → atlas register on green for closed-form-atom-shaped kinds (skip for observation-only) → aggregate ledger at exports/sweep/<batch>/. Inverse of one-big-run: N small verify-able experiments in parallel; honest sweep (FALSIFIED is CLOSED-negative, never skipped — g63). 0.4.0 lands the domain-agnostic generalization: kind is ABSTRACT (materials wall · LLM bench · web smoke · build bench are all examples among many; no kind hard-coded as default), Stage 1.5 infra existence check (verify the four generic prereqs runnable+inputs+parser+workdir per candidate; HALT with N/M candidates missing dispatch infrastructure — /cycle-bg <active-domain> required first when any miss), and a /cycle-bg ↔ /micro-exp decision tree cross-linked in both SKILL.md (BUILD lane vs FIRE lane; mirrors skills/cycle/SKILL.md). Local-pool adapter generalized — works on ANY kind that has a runnable + parser. Bare = current context · arg = scope filter. Distinct from /cycle (milestone-driven loop) and /all-bg-go (reactive single fan-out). NL trigger via skill (sweep these, verify-driven sweep, build then fire, infra missing).

plugins.name: stdlib-ssot-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit) + SessionStart advisory for commons @D g61 (hexa-lang stdlib = SSOT for shared code, primitives AND domain engines), in hexa-lang (invoked via `hexa run`). NON-BLOCKING (additionalContext only). On a `.hexa` edit it nudges when content fights single-SSOT: (a) abs-path import of another repo's `.hexa` (anima-locked) — promote to stdlib + import "stdlib/…"; (b) defining a `fn NAME` that already exists as a `pub fn` in hexa-lang stdlib (duplicate) — import it (best-effort grep, fail-safe). SessionStart checks the single physical stdlib root (~/core/hexa-lang/stdlib). Edits under hexa-lang/stdlib skipped. Pairs with the /stdlib skill.

plugins.name: stdlib
plugins.description: /stdlib — hexa-lang stdlib SSOT helper (commons @D g61). `check` (bare) scans the cwd repo for g61 violations: abs-path cross-repo `.hexa` imports + local fn names duplicating a hexa-lang stdlib `pub fn`. `promote <file>` prints the move-to-stdlib + thin-shim runbook (engine⊥adapter; the phi_native / IIT4 #1051 flow). Read-only scan; promote prints a runbook. Pairs with the stdlib-ssot-guard hook.
